rect.data-narration: Unix Files are the basic building blocks of the file system. Understanding how to name files is essential to navigating and managing the file system effectively.

text.data-narration: In this section, we will explore the basics of naming files in Unix.

rect.data-narration: Unix files are case-sensitive and must follow certain conventions to ensure compatibility and avoid errors.
The maximum length of a file name in Unix is 255 characters.
File names with spaces or special characters must be enclosed in quotes.
Reserved names cannot be used for user files.
Use descriptive and unique names for files.
Avoid using special characters and spaces in file names.
Use underscores to separate words in file names.
Use a consistent naming convention throughout the file system.
Use descriptive file names that indicate the purpose of the file.
Use file extensions to indicate the type of file.
The file name example.txt indicates that it is a text file.
The file name script.sh indicates that it is a shell script.
The file name document.docx indicates that it is a Microsoft Word document.
